content: If we adapt the human language learning pedagogy of repeated guided reflection practice (from the high school English vocabulary curriculum interpretation of Reflexion) to train LLM self-reflection mechanisms, we can help large language models internalize consistent self-reflection routines rather than relying on one-off inconsistent perspective reflection tricks. This turns LLM self-reflection from a niche corrective post-processing step into a foundational, generalizable skill analogous to how humans learn to reflect on their own writing and language use.
